channel.title: Comments for page "End of Death Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "End of Death Hub"

item.title: Question: What about simple cremation?
item.content:encoded: I'm having a hard time understanding why in this scenario cremation is not a considered option, did I miss something?

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image used in this article has been removed for non-compliance with the SCP Wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). Please forward all questions and/or concerns to ProcyonLotor.

EDIT: The image has been replaced and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favourite canons on the wiki and I'd love to write something for it. However, I don't want whatever I write to be out of place here; Is there a writer's guide for this hub? I don't want to misconstruct EOD'S canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: soon. gabe and i have a draft of the final tale but it needs more work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I adore this entire hub, but will the finale to the persephone project ever be finished? I've been waiting for a long time for one of my favorite scp canons to be finished.

item.title: End of death, not end of hunger
item.content:encoded:
Hey so I'm in the process of writing a tale exploring how the Foundation's food services would change in the world of End of Death. So far I've come up with these ideas:
1. Even though people can no longer die of starvation, they obviously still require food to get anything done.
2. Eating meat is no longer an option. People likely wouldn't eat a steak if they knew the cow is still alive somewhere.
3. The new best source of protein is eggs, resulting in most meals using them.
4. Since fungi aren't affected by ΩK, yeast can still be used in baking.
5. Plants aren't affected either, so fruits, vegetables, sugar etc. are still used.

Has anyone else thought about this aspect of the world?

item.title: Could I write an explaination for SCP-008? (aka the zombie virus)
item.content:encoded: I think it'd be interesting to explore how the zombie virus works on people that can't die.

item.title: Re: A question
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure the former way has been done for ages, but whether it gets linked to on this hub is entirely up to whoever's maintaining the hub.

item.title: A question
item.content:encoded: Are users from other branches allowed to write "end-of-death" stories within their own branch, or they have to translate it to English and get it published on English branch first?

item.title: Re: This is really good but I only just realized Bioprinting ruins it all.
item.content:encoded:
Fair.

What's this canon's answer to that?

I'd love to see that answer. Would you consider writing a tale?

item.title: Re: This is really good but I only just realized Bioprinting ruins it all.
item.content:encoded:
Um… Living things? Omega-K doesn't prevent life. It only prevents brain death. How would eating work if the cells can't die? I mean they previously stated in these comments that cells can die as an answer to why everything isn't tumors…

What?

item.title: Re: This is really good but I only just realized Bioprinting ruins it all.
item.content:encoded: Where are you getting the living cells from?

item.title: This is really good but I only just realized Bioprinting ruins it all.
item.content:encoded:
Basically Bioprinting is a real world concept like 3D printing where we take living cells and artificially assemble them into working organs. Even in real life we're getting close to cracking it. Basically my deal is that if they can literally implant brains into other bodies and brains into robots and all this crazy crap with anderson and the sci-fi-esque foundation why couldn't they(anybody really) just figure out bioprinting? Why would kidnapping kids even be (majoritarily) in the question if you can just print off an arm or two or even a new body? What's this canon's answer to that?

Also what about SCP-008? Is it just a cold or something now?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Both good questions!

As for seagull's, we address the bugs in Population Control(led).

And regarding cells we have the following from SCP-3984 :

Currently, all known lifeforms in the kingdom Animalia are under the effects of SCP-3984

This is largely because we define death has having to do with brain function, so things like cells don't have brains, therefore End of Death technically doesn't apply to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Follow-up question: what about cells? Shouldn't everything be a tumor-riddled mess if none of their cells can die?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Question: what about bugs? I’d think that, if everything stopped dying, bugs would overrun the world way before humans could.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
Congrats on this hub being in the Top Rated Pages of All Time

+20 tops was a simpler time

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Severity: Survivable"

You are technically correct - the best kind of correct.

+1 (also, the banner is cool)

P.S. That is not dead which can eternal lie, and after strange aeons even death may die.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Go cast your fancy magic someplace else." +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I must be a lightweight. I honestly can’t read this canon. It’s well written, but it just feels depressing and unpleasant in a non-cathartic sense. Obviously I don’t read a setting that features child rape and mass murder to get uplifting stories, but stuff about animals with their guts spilling out and filled with maggots yet unable to die and the Foundation being so evil even the chaos insurgency is sympathetic compared to them makes it feel, to me, just unpleasant to read.

Again, not dissing the writing or anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically, there is no canon, but only in that there are infinite canons. Every reader and writer has their own version of "The SCP Foundation", in that everybody has their own unique opinion as to how they believe the Foundation would operate (called their "headcanon"), as well as their stances on specific topics, the existence of specific departments, the existence of certain SCPs, how amnestics work, etc. In addition to headcanons, sometimes a group of writers will come together to write articles and tales that follow a specific storyline that takes place in a very specific iteration of the Foundation universe. End of Death is one of those canons, where all of the authors are writing within a universe where everything with a nervous system is incapable of dying. There are others, like a canon that focuses entirely on building lore around a boring SCP, or a canon that deals entirely with a Foundation site placed in the middle of a not-so-quiet Midwestern town, and all of them are quite the read, if you have the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Welcome to the scp wiki where there are more canons and somewhat confusing storylines than your mind can handle.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi, sorry… I'm new to the site and digging through the dauntingly vast archive of stories and SCP's can be confusing to me sometimes.

Did this actually happen? As in, is this canon? I'm thinking the answer is no, seeing as I remember reading SCP articles that take place after the "End of Death" date and recall plenty of people dying. In which case I'm guessing this is non-canon, unless I'm mistaken.

Or does this site have several different canons?

Edit: Yeah there are different canons and oh my god what have I gotten myself into.

item.title: Re: Petition to make EoD a full fledged canon?
item.content:encoded: i dont think its on the dropdown anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: that would kind of ruin it though. it needs to stay o n earth so this canon can survive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you think about it, this is a horrible situation. Unless humanity is the only race in the universe (which I'd say is unlikely according to all the bloody outer space SCPs) and we can spread out by colonizing planets.

item.title: Re: Petition to make EoD a full fledged canon?
item.content:encoded:
Now I can finally write a EOD tale. I probably will never get it.
E: will there be a writing guide?

item.title: Re: Petition to make EoD a full fledged canon?
item.content:encoded: Well, that's what I get for not checking before I commented I guess! Glad to see this canon getting the recognition it deserves!

item.title: Re: Petition to make EoD a full fledged canon?
item.content:encoded: Well, I have good news for you! End of Death was inducted into official canon hood yesterday! It's on both the drop down, and toward the bottom of the canon hub page.

item.title: Petition to make EoD a full fledged canon?
item.content:encoded:
This page should totally be added to the canon hub. End of Death has:

  • More than 5 articles written
  • More than 3 contributing authors
  • I'll go out on a limb here and assume that the canon isn't closed off to just the people who have already contributed, but correct me if I am wrong about that.
  • We're in the middle of season 2, so it is currently ongoing.

EoD checks all the necessary boxes. I say it's time to try and get on the hub.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He's solved it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
just turn off peaceful mode

Joking aside I love this series.

item.title: This team has done incredible work.
item.content:encoded: This…mini canon is like a more fluid text-based version of Sunday Without God easier without all the obnoxious ambiguity and characters that. Or an easier to read Death with Interruptions but with a truly globalized perspective. The characters owever briefly they appear, make me care about their fates. My only complaint was seeing Manna Charitable being treated like a complete joke when they would probably be the ones reacting with the most relief and/horror and may.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Have you guys seen Torchwood? The entirety of season 5 (Miracle Day) deals with this concept.
There people stop dying but can still get sick/injured/… but only humans.

Spoilers ahead.

+ show block
– hide block

They don't go quite as in depth as you guys with how it would play out long term, but since there isn't as much paranormal stuff the governments take other actions, a lot of asian countries sterilize the population with drugs in the water and the US and UK start death/incineration camps for the invalid.
There's more stuff they explore (How healthcare would be re-prioritized changes in the justice system,..) but it's been years since I watched it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How dare you have such a cool banner. How *dare* you.
I expected this hub page to reach a cap of like +20 honestly

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A solid layout for a hub, but the image placement and how big it is absolutely makes it jarring as fuck. I think repositioning it and resizing it would make it better.

item.title: Best Thing In Years
item.content:encoded:
This is one of the best thing I've read in months on this site. I'm shocked, impressed, and giddy that you made death — in this case 'not-death' — fucking terrifying. I was a little disappointed at the ending (More O5 development and tying up the loose ends would have made this more power) but goddamn, I will not deny this is some fine ass fucking writing.

+1. +1 again and again and again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: (As one of the writers, part of my reason to want to do this was a love for 17776)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. People hates immortality so hard that Prometheus and MC&D gain money from people that desperately need want to die. I want to hear something from a religion leader regarding this "immortality" event, maybe from Catholic Pope or Islamic Imam. Very interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
if you guys dig the whole "humans delaing with imortality" kinda deal without all the grimdarkness of the Foundation, check out "what will football look like in the future?" by SBNation.
just read a bit and then some HTML screwyness will kick in, the ensuing story is pretty nice.

item.title: Great job guys!
item.content:encoded: GOD I love these! I’m so excited that this contest has brought so many great stories! So far I think this one has my vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the perfect idea for this contest. It relates to everything people have feared at one point in their life, and a thought everyone has had at one point. Death and immortality. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, it's too big. It stretches over the left-hand section. You don't really ever see that on this site, and it's really distracting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*popcorn intensifies*

Also I totally miscounted there’s 13 slots go me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy cow are you guys really gonna have twelve total articles??

+ show block
– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Holy cow are you guys really gonna have twelve total articles?? I’m gonna need some popcorn.

Out of all of the entries thus far I really like this one the best-I didn’t realize until now just how terrifying immortality can be, especially with the insect populations growing immensely. *shudder* I can feel them crawling on my back just thinking about it.

Best of luck to you guys, can’t wait to read the full “plot” when it’s complete,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's certainly different than the normal style but I think it helps get the reader's attention well and is generally cool looking. I get where you are coming from, but I can't see any issues with it myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I disagree, I like it. Its bold. Not seeing any formatting issues with it either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The placement of the image above the entire thing looks very strange when it should be placed more with the text on the right than above. It looks like an error in one's Internet connection or programming. The text "Artwork" somewhat overlaps a little on the image which makes it look even further like an error.

I think it is best for it to be beneath the title which many other pages have done. For example, like how the image in the Dread & Circuses hub is placed (also drawn by Zhange).

Also, you're welcome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the comment is appreciated, your criticism is not really very helpful - what exactly is wrong with it, what could be done to make it better by your standards?

(thanks for picking up that comma by the way)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image needs to be reformatted. It just does not look good where it is at.

item.title: Co-author Post · End of Death Hub
item.content:encoded:
Hub page for team "I don't wanna die" for the 2018 Doomsday Contest.

Our articles are split into two sections: articles that can be considered to be the 'plot' of ΩK, and articles that explore how the world reacts to it.

Thank you to Aethris for the new artwork!
